heroHeadline: RUTTE IN ANKARA FOR JULY SUMMIT PREP; TRUMP: NATO 'ABSOLUTELY USELESS'; STOLTENBERG: 'NOT WRITTEN IN STONE'
heroSubtitle: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte arrived in Ankara on April 21 for a closed two-day meeting with President Erdoğan, FM Fidan, and Defense Minister Güler — preparing the July 7–8 NATO Leaders' Summit in Turkey amid the deepest US-alliance crisis in history. The visit follows Trump's declaration that NATO is 'absolutely useless' and 'STAY AWAY' on Truth Social (April 18), after the Strait of Hormuz reopened via ceasefire. Former Secretary General Stoltenberg warned April 15 that 'it is not a natural law that NATO will exist for the next ten years.' Rutte countered in Welt am Sonntag (April 18) that US withdrawal is 'unfounded speculation' and the nuclear umbrella 'remains the absolute guarantor of security in Europe.' UK PM Starmer committed to raising defense spending to 2.6% GDP by 2027 and 3% by 2029–2034. Hungary's Péter Magyar (Tisza), elected April 13, is prepared to rebuild Budapest's NATO alignment ahead of the Helsingborg Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22). All 32 NATO allies now meet the 2% GDP threshold for the first time in alliance history.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour, Gulf News, US Senate Foreign Relations Committee, GB News, Welt am Sonntag, Turkish Minute, CFR
